course_title: Occupational Therapy Practice in Community Contexts
course_description:
This case-based course will provide occupational therapy students with the skills to enhance client participation across different legislative and practice environments. It will equip students to work with individual clients, organisations and communities. Through reflection and student discussion around cases, clinical decision making will be enhanced.

This pre-placement course is designed to further develop occupational therapists-in-training’s capacity to implement, integrate and build upon previously acquired occupational therapy knowledge and skills in preparation for practice within the dynamic community context that is largely influenced by Australian Disability, Health and Social Care systems.

In particular, the course emphasises choice, control and inclusion within a person/consumer-centred approach to occupational therapy practice across diverse community-based contexts.

A strong focus is placed on applied clinical skills required for safe and effective practice , including:

There are weekly preparation activities to be completed prior to workshops . Occupational Therapists-in-training are expected to demonstrate professional conduct by engaging fully in these preparatory tasks, consistent with expectations for preparation in professional practice placements.

Workshops are delivered in an applied format , enabling participants to consolidate knowledge and develop practical skills in service planning and provision across a diverse range of contemporary practice areas, including home modifications, assistive technology, community assessment and intervention, private practice models, and working within current disability, health, and aged-care schemes and reforms.

Occupational therapists-in-training will be required to actively draw on knowledge and skills acquired from previously completed occupational therapy and broader university coursework to enhance integration, clinical reasoning, and readiness for professional practice.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

A detailed course timetable
outlining
all commitments (preparation activities, tutorials and assessment tasks)
including those that are
compulsory,
will be made available via the
OCTY3104 Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
Occupational Therapists-in-training are expected to review this regularly and plan accordingly.
course_aims: The purpose of this course is to provide students with the professional knowledge and skills base required for practice across diverse community-based contexts, particularly focusing on responding to the dynamic shifts in the Australian Health and Social Care Systems (i.e. National Disability Insurance Scheme and reforms to community aged care). It is concentrated on addressing person/consumer-centred needs within home and community environments while considering the impacts of organisational and pragmatic factors. This course emphasises the importance of acknowledging the consumers' right to choice, control, and inclusion within occupational therapy practice.

learning_outcomes.description: Determine the impact of legislative and pragmatic factors as they apply to occupational therapy in different community-based practice contexts.
Evaluate the impact of the environment on occupations and participation for diverse clinical populations.

Execute a systematic and reasoned occupational therapy process to assess, create, and evaluate environment-based interventions focused on the use and appropriate application of home modifications and assistive technology.
Use ethical and professional practice, employing occupational therapy skills, techniques, and practice processes to plan and provide services to both individuals and organisations.

Analyse and implement people handling and measurement skills within service provision and evaluate and justify clinical reasoning and risk management decisions to ensure safe and effective practice in clinical settings.
Apply effective communication strategies to present clinical recommendations and justifications to clients and relevant stakeholders and facilitate associated group discussions.
Reflect on and provide critical appraisal of own and others' performance and implement appropriate changes in response to this appraisal.
Create an inclusive learning community that supports own and peer/colleague knowledge acquisition and skill development as relevant to practice in community-based contexts.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This exam will be conducted face-to-face, on-campus (St Lucia) and will be completed electronically using Inspera with Safe Exam Browser. Occupational therapists-in-training will be expected to prepare and bring their own device on which to complete the exam. If Queensland Health restrictions preclude on-campus assessment, this exam will be conducted as an online non-invigilated exam for all students on the same date and time as previously scheduled.
Alternative Exam Arrangements can be made (and applied to in-semester exams) in specific circumstances. Please see information on circumstances and how to apply at Alternative Exam Arrangements (https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/alternative-exam-arrangements). Exam adjustments are separate to a Student Access Plan (Disability). Your SAP does NOT apply to exams. This only covers in-class participation, activities, and assessment. If you have approved Alternative Exam Arrangements and you have a course with school-based examinations (e.g. mid-semester exams, in-class quizzes, online quizzes, tutorial exercises etc.) then please advise the course coordinator and examinations of the adjustments and assessments respectively as soon as possible to increase the potential for arrangements to be accommodated.
Generative AI (ChatGPT) - This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request.
Late submission
Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .
Late submissions will not be permitted for this assessment as the times are set within the Inspera platform.

assessment_details.title: Case-Based Series Assessment - Submission 1: Presentation & Facilitated Discussion
assessment_details.due_date: The Case-Based Series Assessment is a "Series Assessment" and the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion is the First Submission in the series. 5/05/2026 - 8/05/2026 The Case-Based Series Assessment is a "Series Assessment" and the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion is the First Submission in the series. All occupational therapists-in-training, regardless of the date for their Presentation and Facilitated Discussion session, will have to submit their PowerPoint presentation on Tuesday, 5th of May 2026 at 9:00am and also finalise their Buddycheck by Friday 8th May 2026 at 4:00pm . Please note that while this submission is a Pass/Fail item and the first submission in the Case-Based Series Assessment, it is not a MUST PASS submission and occupational therapists-in-training can still PASS the Case-Based Series Assessment even if they receive a FAIL for the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion submission. As a MUST PASS assessment item, occupational therapists-in-training must receive a PASS on the Case-Based Series Assessment to PASS OCTY3104.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Occupational therapists-in-training will work in groups (size to be determined based on tutorial numbers, however, groups of 4/5 students likely) to identify a home environment to review for their presentation and case-based assignment. Presentations will be held in Week 10, across the four block times (i.e. Tuesday 12:00am - 2:00pm, Tuesday 2:00pm - 4:00pm, Wednesday 1:00pm - 3:00pm and Wednesday 3:00pm - 5:00pm). Students will only be required to attend one (1) presentation session . In this 20 minute presentation students will: 1) Briefly present two (2) designated areas of the environment with pictures, including features and specifications. 2) Summarise preliminary recommendations for possible home modifications, assistive technology and related strategies to support goals/performance/participation/needs of the service consumer and succinctly present their clinical justification/reasoning for these recommendations. 3) Facilitate a discussion amongst their peers with regard to the recommendations/justification, aiming to expand/improve/refine/evaluate their preliminary thoughts in preparation for the completion of the case-based assignment report. Pass/Fail Requirements (Hurdles) To successfully be able to complete this submission in the case-based series assessment all students must: 1) Individual Hurdle: Attend a compulsory service consumer session during class time on Monday 20th April 2026 from 11:00am - 1:00pm OR 1:00pm - 3:00pm. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend this session and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have received and engaged with the necessary case-based series assessment-related information that was gathered within the session. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 27th of April 2026 @ 4:00pm . 2) Group Hurdle: Submit an electronic copy (1 per group) of the PowerPoint presentation (including all pictures) for the presentation and facilitated discussion online by Tuesday, 5th of May 2026 at 9:00am . 3) Individual Hurdle: Attend (compulsory), in full, all presentations and facilitated discussions for their Week 10 session on Tuesday 5th May 2026 OR Wednesday 6th May 2026 and actively participate in professional colleague evaluations (within own group) for all groups that present, submitting the group evaluation online. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend their session in full and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have reviewed the missed presentations/facilitated discussions and have reflected upon and evaluated the recommendations/clinical justifications within each of the missed presentations. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 18th of May 2026 @ 4:00pm . 4) Individual Hurdle: Complete a formative Buddycheck for each member of their group, including themselves, to record the contributions of each of the group member to the presentation and facilitated discussion submission. These are due by Friday 8th May 2026 at 4:00pm at the latest for all students. A fail on any of the above items (i.e., not participating and not completing a make-up activity) may bring about a fail for the corresponding submission: a) If fail attending session with service consumer then may fail the subsequent case-based series assessment presentation and facilitated discussion, report, and clinical supervision viva. b) If fail to submit the PowerPoint presentation or attend all group presentations for the relevant session then may fail the case-based series assessment presentation and facilitated discussion. The exception to this is the Buddycheck which is a formative peer assessment item that is used with the intention of practicing providing/receiving feedback to/from group members about their performance and contributions within the group. Professional Behaviour Code 1) Occupational therapists-in-training are expected to be respectful at all times when in contact with the service consumer details and to treat all case material with the privacy and confidentiality that would be expected by OT clinical practice (i.e. all notes, recordings, assessment forms etc. pertaining to this case should be stored securely and not shared with anyone outside the OCTY3104 course). 2) Occupational therapists-in-training are expected to work collaboratively in a group and contribute equitably throughout the whole process for this submission in the case-based series assessment. 3) A consent form must be acquired for any photos/video taken of specific individuals if necessary. 4) Please refer to the Australian Health Practitioners Regulation Agency (AHPRA) - Code of Conduct for Occupational Therapists (Particularly - Section 3.4: Confidentiality and Privacy) at https://www.occupationaltherapyboard.gov.au/Codes-Guidelines/Code-of-conduct.aspx for expectations regarding professional behaviour. The case material, including the task description, marking criteria, and supporting resources, will be available to students via Learn.UQ (Blackboard). Generative AI (e.g., ChatGPT/Gamma) - This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted for the presentation or the creation of any affiliated resources. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Criteria & Marking: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access marking criteria held in this profile. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submissions :
O ccupational therapists-in-training will submit their PowerPoint (1 per group), Professional Colleague Evaluations (1 per group), and Buddycheck (1 per student) via online submission. The Presentation and Facilitated Discussion will be submitted at the allocated time in-person within the relevant presentation session.
Feedback :
Feedback for the Case-Based Series Assessment Presentation and Facilitated Discussion preliminary recommendations will be provided to students verbally during the sessions. Additionally, a written summary will later be provided via Learn.UQ, and final results will be available in Grade Centre once marking is complete.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
No extensions can be requested for this assessment item. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend their presentation session in full and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e., medical, or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete an alternative assessment make-up activity to ensure that they are familiar with the content that was presented on their behalf by their group and that they have reviewed the missed presentations and reflected upon and evaluated the recommendations/clinical justifications within each of the missed presentations. This alternative assessment make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 18th of May 2026 @ 4:00pm.
Late submission
As a PASS/FAIL submission there is no penalty for Late Submission. If occupational therapists-in-training, however, do not complete the submission any of the relevant hurdles then they are at risk of receiving a FAIL for the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion submission.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: As a PASS/FAIL submission there is no penalty for Late Submission. If occupational therapists-in-training, however, do not complete the submission any of the relevant hurdles then they are at risk of receiving a FAIL for the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion submission.

assessment_details.title: Case-Based Series Assessment - Submission 2: Report
assessment_details.due_date: 25/05/2026 4:00 pm The Case-Based Series Assessment is a "Series Assessment" and the Report is the Second Submission in the series. Please note that while this submission is weighted (40%), it is also a MUST PASS submission and occupational therapists-in-training will need to PASS the Case-Based Series Assessment Report in order to PASS the Case-Based Series Assessment overall. As a MUST PASS assessment item, occupational therapists-in-training must receive a PASS on the Case-Based Series Assessment to PASS OCTY3104.
assessment_details.task_description: Occupational therapists-in-training will work in pairs (or groups of three where necessary) on a Case-Based Series Assessment Report which requires them to utilise knowledge and practical skills acquired through this and previous courses to make recommendations and provide clinical justification/reasoning to support the goals/performance/participation/needs of a service consumer in their home environment and community. To achieve this occupational therapists-in-training will have to: 1) Critically review the case materials, attend a compulsory service consumer session, and participate in the group presentation and facilitated discussion submission and associated activities (including assessment of a home environment, presentation of preliminary recommendations/justification, and completion of a facilitated peer discussion). 2) Apply their knowledge, practical skills, reasoning, and points gathered from the presentation and facilitated discussion (including provided feedback) to determine final feasible recommendations for home modifications, assistive technology, and related strategies to meet the service consumer requirements within the relevant home environment. 3) Produce a consumer-friendly and funder-appropriate home modifications/assistive technology report with four (4) to six (6) computer drawings (completed in an approved program) of the two (2) designated areas pre and post-modification (there must be at least one (1) pre and one (1) post drawing for each area) and a maximum of two (2) standard A4 pages of assistive technology recommendation pictures (pictures and labels ONLY). This report must consider and demonstrate reference to the relevant literature, evidence, case information, and practice context. The final report submission is due on Monday 25th May 2026 at 4:00pm (16:00) and should be 1.15 spaced and a maximum of 4 standard A4 pages, (not including drawings or pictures). In-text citations and the reference list should be consistent with APA 7th and the in-text citation will be included as part of the 4 standard A4 pages page limit, however, the reference list, like the drawings and pictures, will be considered as additional to the 4 pages of the report. PLEASE NOTE: All drawings and pictures need to be referred to in the main body of the report. Pass/Fail Requirements (Hurdles) To successfully be able to complete this submission in the case-based series assessment all occupational therapists-in-training must: 1) Individual Hurdle: Attend a compulsory service consumer session during class time on Monday 20th April 2026 from 11:00am - 1:00pm OR 1:00pm - 3:00pm. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend this session and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have received and engaged with the necessary case-based series assessment-related information that was gathered within the session. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 27th of April 2026 @ 4:00pm . A fail on the above item (i.e., not participating and not completing a make-up activity) may bring about a fail for the corresponding submission: a) If fail attending session with service consumer then may fail the subsequent case-based series assessment presentation and facilitated discussion, report, and clinical supervision viva. O ccupational therapists-in-training should also note that as a series assessment, they must complete all submissions in the series, therefore to be eligible to pass the Report submission, students will need to have completed the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion Submission. Generative AI (e.g., ChatGPT) Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (e.g., ChatGPT, Microsoft CoPilot, & Claude) are increasingly used in society. For writing clinical reports in this course, students may not use AI unless explicitly permitted by the course coordinator. If AI use is allowed, the specific guidelines must be clearly defined before implementation to ensure its acceptability and address any potential risks. AI is not a replacement of, or substitute for, professional reasoning or clinical skills. Human oversight is essential in its use, and to employ generative AI effectively and responsibly, students must understand how AI works, including its functions, data storage methods, and its impact on adherence to professional codes of conduct. If used during report writing, occupational therapists-in-training are responsible for any outputs generated by AI and will be held accountable for the actions of any AI that is used in practice/report preparation. This responsibility includes ensuring adherence to the relevant profession's code of conduct and ethical standards. Transparency about generative AI use is essential and occupational therapists-in-training must identify, document, and submit how the AI was used in the writing of this report (submitted as an additional report section - in addition to outlined page limit) and must follow the relevant policies and procedures of The University of Queensland and the OCTY3104 course. Occupational therapists-in-training are reminded that they need to demonstrate their ability to meet the inherent requirements of the occupational therapy profession, operate within its scope of practice, and ensure that client/consumer/patient privacy and confidentiality are maintained at all times. Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical or client/consumer/patient information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access marking criteria held in this profile. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission :
Students to make their submission (1 per pair/group) online by 4:00pm (16:00hrs) on the due date.
Feedback :
Feedback for the Case-Based Series Assessment Report will be provided via comments on the report and associated marking aligned with the marking criteria rubric. This will all be provided via Learn.UQ, and final results will be available in Grade Centre once marking is complete.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request via my.UQ > my.UQ Dashboard > my. Requests and provide evidence of circumstance, as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension is listed on the my.UQ website: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Late submission will result in a 10% reduction of the maximum mark for the Case-Based Series Assessment Report, applied to the student's final score. Occupational therapists-in-training are reminded that this is a MUST PASS item (including hurdles) within the Case-Based Series Assessment, and therefore, if occupational therapists-in-training FAIL this Report, they will fail the Case-Based Series Assessment overall.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.ai_statement:
Occupational therapists-in-training will work in pairs (or groups of three where necessary) on a Case-Based Series Assessment Report which requires them to utilise knowledge and practical skills acquired through this and previous courses to make recommendations and provide clinical justification/reasoning to support the goals/performance/participation/needs of a service consumer in their home environment and community. To achieve this occupational therapists-in-training will have to: 1) Critically review the case materials, attend a compulsory service consumer session, and participate in the group presentation and facilitated discussion submission and associated activities (including assessment of a home environment, presentation of preliminary recommendations/justification, and completion of a facilitated peer discussion). 2) Apply their knowledge, practical skills, reasoning, and points gathered from the presentation and facilitated discussion (including provided feedback) to determine final feasible recommendations for home modifications, assistive technology, and related strategies to meet the service consumer requirements within the relevant home environment. 3) Produce a consumer-friendly and funder-appropriate home modifications/assistive technology report with four (4) to six (6) computer drawings (completed in an approved program) of the two (2) designated areas pre and post-modification (there must be at least one (1) pre and one (1) post drawing for each area) and a maximum of two (2) standard A4 pages of assistive technology recommendation pictures (pictures and labels ONLY). This report must consider and demonstrate reference to the relevant literature, evidence, case information, and practice context. The final report submission is due on Monday 25th May 2026 at 4:00pm (16:00) and should be 1.15 spaced and a maximum of 4 standard A4 pages, (not including drawings or pictures). In-text citations and the reference list should be consistent with APA 7th and the in-text citation will be included as part of the 4 standard A4 pages page limit, however, the reference list, like the drawings and pictures, will be considered as additional to the 4 pages of the report. PLEASE NOTE: All drawings and pictures need to be referred to in the main body of the report. Pass/Fail Requirements (Hurdles) To successfully be able to complete this submission in the case-based series assessment all occupational therapists-in-training must: 1) Individual Hurdle: Attend a compulsory service consumer session during class time on Monday 20th April 2026 from 11:00am - 1:00pm OR 1:00pm - 3:00pm. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend this session and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have received and engaged with the necessary case-based series assessment-related information that was gathered within the session. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 27th of April 2026 @ 4:00pm . A fail on the above item (i.e., not participating and not completing a make-up activity) may bring about a fail for the corresponding submission: a) If fail attending session with service consumer then may fail the subsequent case-based series assessment presentation and facilitated discussion, report, and clinical supervision viva. O ccupational therapists-in-training should also note that as a series assessment, they must complete all submissions in the series, therefore to be eligible to pass the Report submission, students will need to have completed the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion Submission. Generative AI (e.g., ChatGPT) Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (e.g., ChatGPT, Microsoft CoPilot, & Claude) are increasingly used in society. For writing clinical reports in this course, students may not use AI unless explicitly permitted by the course coordinator. If AI use is allowed, the specific guidelines must be clearly defined before implementation to ensure its acceptability and address any potential risks. AI is not a replacement of, or substitute for, professional reasoning or clinical skills. Human oversight is essential in its use, and to employ generative AI effectively and responsibly, students must understand how AI works, including its functions, data storage methods, and its impact on adherence to professional codes of conduct. If used during report writing, occupational therapists-in-training are responsible for any outputs generated by AI and will be held accountable for the actions of any AI that is used in practice/report preparation. This responsibility includes ensuring adherence to the relevant profession's code of conduct and ethical standards. Transparency about generative AI use is essential and occupational therapists-in-training must identify, document, and submit how the AI was used in the writing of this report (submitted as an additional report section - in addition to outlined page limit) and must follow the relevant policies and procedures of The University of Queensland and the OCTY3104 course. Occupational therapists-in-training are reminded that they need to demonstrate their ability to meet the inherent requirements of the occupational therapy profession, operate within its scope of practice, and ensure that client/consumer/patient privacy and confidentiality are maintained at all times. Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical or client/consumer/patient information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access marking criteria held in this profile. Read full task description Hide full task description
Generative AI (e.g., ChatGPT)
Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (e.g., ChatGPT, Microsoft CoPilot, & Claude) are increasingly used in society.

If used during report writing, occupational therapists-in-training are responsible for any outputs generated by AI and will be held accountable for the actions of any AI that is used in practice/report preparation. This responsibility includes ensuring adherence to the relevant profession's code of conduct and ethical standards. Transparency about generative AI use is essential and occupational therapists-in-training must identify, document, and submit how the AI was used in the writing of this report (submitted as an additional report section - in addition to outlined page limit) and must follow the relevant policies and procedures of The University of Queensland and the OCTY3104 course. assessment_details.title: Case-based Series Assessment - Submission 3: Clinical Supervision Viva
assessment_details.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.due_date: End of Semester Exam Period 6/06/2026 - 20/06/2026 This Case-based Series Assessment Clinical Supervision Viva will take place during the End of Semester Exam period and specified days and times will be scheduled and advised once the Central Examination Timetable has been publicly released. The Case-Based Series Assessment is a "Series Assessment" and the Clinical Supervision Viva is the Third Submission in the series. Please note that while this submission is weighted (30%), it is also a MUST PASS submission and occupational therapists-in-training will need to PASS the Case-Based Series Assessment Clinical Supervision Viva in order to PASS the Case-Based Series Assessment overall. As a MUST PASS assessment item, occupational therapists-in-training must receive a PASS on the Case-Based Series Assessment to PASS OCTY3104.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Occupational therapists-in-training will participate in an in-person 30-minute individual clinical supervision viva examination to discuss their Case-Based Series Report and associated clinical activities. The viva will assess abilities to discuss and justify responses to feedback, articulate clinical and professional reasoning, evaluate intervention outcomes, and demonstrate understanding of interprofessional communication and working with construction professionals, assistive technology providers, and related clinical services in community practice. Occupational therapists-in-training should be prepared to critically reflect on their clinical decision-making, presentation/report feedback, and articulate their future actions as an occupational therapist-in-training and to justify their therapeutic and clinical approaches across their case series. Pass/Fail Requirements (Hurdles) To successfully be able to complete this submission in the case-based series assessment, all occupational therapists-in-training must: 1) Individual Hurdle: Attend a compulsory service consumer session during class time on Monday 20th April 2026 from 11:00am - 1:00pm OR 1:00pm - 3:00pm. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend this session and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have received and engaged with the necessary case-based series assessment-related information that was gathered within the session. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 27th of April 2026 @ 4:00pm . A fail on the above item (i.e., not participating and not completing a make-up activity) may bring about a fail for the corresponding submission: a) If fail attending session with service consumer then may fail the subsequent case-based series assessment presentation and facilitated discussion, report, and clinical supervision viva. Occupational therapists-in-training should also note that, as a series assessment, they must complete all submissions in the series to be eligible to pass the Clinical Supervision Viva submission. Occupational therapists-in-training will need to have completed the Presentation and Facilitated Discussion AND Report Submissions. Alternative Exam Arrangements can be made (and applied to in-semester exams) in specific circumstances. Please see information on circumstances and how to apply at Alternative Exam Arrangements (https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/alternative-exam-arrangements). Exam adjustments are separate to a Student Access Plan (Disability). Your SAP does NOT apply to exams. This only covers in-class participation, activities, and assessment. If you have approved Alternative Exam Arrangements and you have a course with school-based examinations (e.g. mid-semester exams, in-class quizzes, online quizzes, tutorial exercises, vivas etc.) then please advise the course coordinator and examinations of the adjustments and assessments respectively as soon as possible to increase the potential for arrangements to be accommodated. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. When an occupational therapist-in-training is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. (Assessment Procedure (95)) Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access marking criteria held in this profile. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time no planning time minutes Duration 20 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - specified written materials permitted Materials Occupational therapists-in-training may bring a copy of their Case-Based Series Assessment Presentation/Facilitated Discussion Slides, Report and associated feedback to support discussion. No additional notes or course content should be brought into the clinical supervision viva. Exam platform Other Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission :
The clinical supervision viva will be submitted in-person on the assigned day at the allocated time.
Feedback :
Feedback for the Case-Based Series Assessment Clinical Supervision Viva will NOT be provided to students verbally during the submission. A written summary and marking aligned with the marking criteria rubric will be provided once marking is finalised and moderated and after final course grades are released. Feedback will be provided via Learn.UQ and final results will be available in Grade Centre after final grade release.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request.
Late submission
Late submissions will not be permitted for this assessment as it is submitted in-person at a specific date, time, and location. Occupational therapists-in-training are reminded that this is a MUST PASS item (including hurdles) within the Case-Based Series Assessment, and therefore, if occupational therapists-in-training FAIL this Clinical Supervision Viva, they will fail the Case-Based Series Assessment overall.

Occupational therapists-in-training are reminded that this is a MUST PASS item (including hurdles) within the Case-Based Series Assessment, and therefore, if occupational therapists-in-training FAIL this Clinical Supervision Viva, they will fail the Case-Based Series Assessment overall.

assessment_details.title: Applied Clinical Skills Series Assessment
assessment_details.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.due_date: Occupational therapists-in-training will attend four consecutive sessions within this period as allocated and outlined on Learn.UQ (Blackboard). 27/02/2026 - 29/05/2026 Occupational therapists-in-training will be assessed during the summative simulation-based experiences in the relevant assigned weeks. Please note that while this assessment is a PASS/FAIL assessment, it is also a MUST PASS submission, and students will need to PASS to PASS the OCTY3104 Course.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Occupational therapists-in-training will participate in four sessions comprised of three clinical activities as part of the Summative Simulation Based Experience designed to develop and assess competency in: People handling Measuring people and equipment Measuring environments Clinical reasoning and service provision Risk management in rehabilitation and community settings. Each clinical activity includes a structured briefing , supervised practical experience , and a facilitated debrief . The assessment structure differs between activities: People Handling is comprised of a range of practice clinical scenarios and assessed through a single isolated task within each relevant week. Measuring People and Equipment and Measuring Environments are assessed through case-based simulation experiences, with approximately 45 minutes allocated per case and assessment occurring across the full duration of the case interactions. Four key cases will translate across all three clinical activities to support continuity and progressive application of skills. Details of these cases will be provided via Learn.UQ (Blackboard). Assignment Tasks: To successfully complete this assessment, occupational therapists-in-training must: 1. Attend the Mandatory Applied Clinical Skills Workshop - Wednesday, 25th February 2026 - 1:00pm - 3:00pm OR 3:00pm - 5:00pm. Attend and actively participate in the designated Applied Clinical Skills workshop, which introduces foundational principles for safe people handling, measurement processes, equipment compatibility, environmental assessment, documentation, and risk management. 2. Participate in Allocated Summative Simulation Based Experience sessions Attend and actively engage in all allocated Summative Simulation Based Experience sessions according to group allocation. During these sessions, occupational therapists-in-training will: Learn, practice, and refine people handling techniques. Conduct measurement of body dimensions, equipment specifications, and environmental features. Work collaboratively in small groups. Engage in structured briefing and debriefing discussions. 3. Demonstrate Competency in People Handling Within the designated weekly assessment task, occupational therapists-in-training must: Prepare the environment and equipment safely. Demonstrate appropriate people handling techniques. Apply safe body mechanics and risk management principles. Communicate clearly with clients and team members. Justify technique selection using clinical reasoning. 4. Demonstrate Competency in Measuring People, Equipment and Environments During simulation based experience cases, occupational therapists-in-training must: Prepare appropriately within the allocated planning period. Accurately measure relevant body dimensions, equipment components, and environmental features Analyse compatibility between person, equipment, and environment Communicate effectively with clients and team members. Demonstrate sound clinical reasoning linking measurements to service provision. Document findings clearly, systematically, and professionally. 5. Engage in Structured Documentation and AI-Supported Learning (where applicable) For one designated case within: Measuring People and Equipment; and Measuring Environments Artificial Intelligence is used as a structured learning tool to support documentation development. Occupational therapists-in-training must: Retain full responsibility for clinical reasoning and accuracy. Ensure no confidential or identifiable information is entered into AI tools. Critically review and verify AI-supported outputs. Submit documentation that reflects professional standards and independent judgement. Artificial Intelligence tools are not permitted in People Handling activities. Alternative Examination Arrangements (AEAs) - As this is a simulation practical assessment, Alternative Examination Arrangements will not apply in these circumstances. If occupational therapists-in-training have a Student Access Plan (Disability) that includes relevant accommodations, these accommodations will be considered and applied where possible within the simulation practical assessment. Occupational therapists-in-training, however, will have to demonstrate the necessary skills to ensure they meet the inherent requirements of the course and the occupational therapy profession. Occupational therapists-in-training with accommodations are encouraged to contact the course coordinator directly if they would like to discuss if their SAP (Disability) accommodations can/will be applied. Artificial Intelligence & Machine Translation This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT, other than those being used as part of the simulation activities (i.e., Heidi Health), may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Recording of Assessment: In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date. Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access marking criteria held in this profile. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time no planning time minutes Duration 210 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Open book examination - any written or printed material is permitted; material may be annotated Materials Summative Simulation Based Experience - Clients/Consumers and relevant tools required to complete clinical activities will be provided within the simulations/practical.. examinations Exam platform Other Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission :
The simulation practical assessment will be submitted in-person on the assigned day at the allocated time.
Demonstrate Competency in People Handling
Assessment is based on the discrete task performed within relevant week.
Demonstrate Competency in Measuring People, Equipment, and Environments
During simulation based experience cases, occupational therapists-in-training will spend approximately 45 minutes per case. Performance is assessed across the entire session (i.e., all cases). Within any case, however, the Clinical Educators may request demonstration of specific skills or ask targeted questions to clarify clinical reasoning, risk management decisions, equipment compatibility considerations, environmental analysis, or service provision planning. Assessment therefore, reflects both observed performance and responses to direct requests or questioning.
Feedback
Feedback for the Applied Clinical Skills will be provided across the sessions. Results will NOT be provided to occupational therapists-in-training verbally during the submission. A written summary and marking aligned with the marking criteria rubric will be provided once marking is finalised and moderated. This will be completed once the final group has commenced their assessment series. Feedback will be provided via Learn.UQ and final results will be available in Grade Centre after marking has been completed.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request.
Late submission
Late submissions will not be permitted for this assessment as it is submitted in-person at a specific date, time (i.e., during simulations/practicals) and location. Students are reminded that this is a MUST PASS item for the OCTY3104 course overall.
Mandatory Applied Clinical Skills Workshop - Non-attendance
If occupational therapists in training are unable to attend the Mandatory Applied Clinical Skills Workshop and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e., medical, or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete an alternative make-up activity to ensure that they are familiar with the content that was presented and confirm they are prepared to proceed to the Applied Clinical Skills activities. This make-up activity will need to be completed before commencement of the Applied Clinical Skills activities, and no later than Monday, 23rd of March 2026 @ 4:00pm.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .
assessment_details.late_submission: Late submissions will not be permitted for this assessment as it is submitted in-person at a specific date, time (i.e., during simulations/practicals) and location. Students are reminded that this is a MUST PASS item for the OCTY3104 course overall.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Practical Applied Tutorials Occupational therapists-in-training will actively participate in practical applied workshops, which foster a community of learning in which to practice and enhance knowledge and skill development. Occupational therapists-in-training will attend two x 2 hour workshops each week (either Monday 11:00 -1:00pm and Wednesday 3:00 - 5:00pm OR Monday 1:00 - 3:00pm and Wednesday 1:00 - 3:00pm). All workshops will be delivered face to face (unless identified otherwise by course staff). These workshops will have a practical/case-based focus and will apply knowledge acquired in the professional preparation activities and previous courses. As occupational therapists-in-training are working within learning communities and will be participating in assessment with another member or other members from the same workshop group it is vital that students attend their relevant workshops. Sign-On will be available for these workshops. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Applied Clinical Skills Summative Simulation Based Experience In Week 1, occupational therapists-in-training will be provided with a single opportunity to attend a practical session focused on developing the clinical skills necessary for measuring people, equipment, and environments. Within the session, course staff will support occupational therapists-in-training to gain information and develop skills required to complete these tasks in clinical practice. For this session, attendance is compulsory. Additionally, occupational therapists-in-training will be expected to attend two assigned simulations/practicals (after they have completed their two People Handling sessions) to practice these skills under summative simulated based experience conditions and demonstrate their competency as per the assessment that is outlined in the assessment section of this course profile. For these sessions, attendance is compulsory. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend these sessions in full and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have reviewed the missed content and can apply it clinically (if miss the Week 1 workshop) OR have developed proficiency/competency in applying the skills in practice. If occupational therapists-in-training miss the workshop the make-up activity will need to be submitted by Wednesday, 11th of March @ 4:00pm . If occupational therapists-in-training miss the simulation/practicals the make-up activity will need to be scheduled and advised by the course coordinator, but where possible will take place in either Week 12 or Week 13 of semester. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 13 (02 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Professional Preparation Activities Occupational therapist-in-training will be expected to engage in structured/guided activities (including the below outlined online modules) that serve as an introduction to each weeks topic and provide a base of knowledge on which to build in the applied workshops. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 4 (09 Mar - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: SHRS Online Modules - NDIS & Private Practice Occupational therapists-in-training will complete two online modules which introduce students to the NDIS and Private Practice. The modules are 1) NDIS Module 1 - Understanding the participant's journey through the NDIS (including revision of "Introduction to the NDIS") and 2) Private Practice Module 1 - Introduction to Private Practice. These modules have content, videos, and activities that will support occupational therapists-in-training to expand their knowledge for these two practice contexts. Learning outcomes: L01, L05

Compulsory Session with Service Consumer In Week 8, occupational therapists-in-training will be provided with a single opportunity to attend a service consumer (client/participant) session. This session will be used as an opportunity to provide details of and insights into the service consumer the Case-Based Series Assessment is centred around. Within the session, course staff will support occupational therapists-in-training to gain information about the consumer and the consumer/equipment specifications necessary for the completion of the Case-Based Series Assessment submissions (i.e., Presentation and Facilitated Discussion, Report, and Clinical Supervision Viva) for this course. For this session, attendance is compulsory and will be recorded. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend this session and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have received and engaged with the necessary case-based series assessment-related information that was gathered within the session. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 27th of April 2026 @ 4:00pm (16:00). Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05

Wheelchair and Seating Professional Development This professional development event is a single-day-long opportunity, held on Saturday (9th of May 2026), for occupational therapists-in-training to gain knowledge from and interact with clients/consumers, occupational therapists, suppliers, and equipment and technology related to experiences of using a mobility aid, wheelchair/motorised mobility aid and seating recommendations, completing relevant documentation/scripts and writing associated assistive technology reports. It will include presentations, demonstrations, and opportunities to view and interact with relevant technologies. Information presented at this event is assessable and will not be made available post-completion of the event, therefore, attendance is strongly encouraged. Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Presentation Attendance & Professional Evaluations In Week 10, occupational therapists-in-training must attend (compulsory), in full, all presentations for their Presentation and Facilitated Discussion session on Tuesday 5th May 2026 OR Wednesday 6th May 2026. Sign-on for these sessions will occur in Weeks 2/3. During these presentations students will actively participate in professional colleague evaluations (within own group) for all groups that present, submitting the group evaluation online. If occupational therapists-in-training are not able to attend their session in full and can provide a legitimate reason (i.e. medical or extenuating circumstances) then they must submit an absentee form with associated evidence to the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences Student and Academic Support Unit (enquiries.shrs@uq.edu.au) within three calendar days. If approval is received, occupational therapists-in-training will then need to complete a make-up activity to ensure that they have reviewed the missed presentations/facilitated discussions and have reflected upon and evaluated the recommendations/clinical justifications within each of the missed presentations. This make-up activity will need to be submitted by Monday, 18th of May 2026 @ 4:00pm. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Fitness to Practise Policy School guidelines Your school has additional guidelines you'll need to follow for this course: Resources for Current Students Learning activities
